Defendant Pneumo Abex Llc Sii'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Pneumo Abex LLC Successor in interest to Abex Corporation erroneously sued herein as Pneumo-Abex LLC
Stephen J. Healy, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Andrew Stinson

RULING:
Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC Sii'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC Successor in interest to Abex Corporation erroneously sued herein as Pneumo-Abex LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 3 (battery) and denied as to issues 1 (negligence) and 2( strict liability). Plaintiff's declaration and defendant's GO 129 interrogatory responses creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing friction products attributable to defendant. The objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Defendant B. Panela Drayage Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel B. Hoye, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4055), appearing for defendant / moving party B. Panela Drayage Co.
Stephen J. Healy, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Andrew Stinson

RULING:
Defendant B. Panela Drayage Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment.
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant B. Panella Drayage Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Construing all of the evidence presented by plaintiff liberally, there is insufficient evidence that any retained control by defendant affirmatively contributed to Mr. Stinson's exposure to asbestos. (Millard v. Bisources (2007) 156 Cal. App. 4th 1338; Michael v. Denbeste Transportation (2006) 137 Cal. App. 4th 1082; Park v. Burlington Northern (2003) 108 Cal. App. 4th 595). Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Steven Ross Scott, Esq. of BURNHAM BROWN (510-835-6822), appearing for defendant BORG-WARNER CORPORATION by its successor-in-interest BORG WARNER MORSE TEC, INC.
Stephen J. Healy, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Andrew Stinson

RULING:
Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation's Motion For Summary Judgment.
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation by its successor-in-interest BORG WARNER MORSE TEC, INC.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's declaration creates a triable fact whether he was to asbestos-containing clutches attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of making a prima facie showing that: 1) Mr. Stinson does not have an asbestos-related disease and 2) Mr. Stinson's exposure to defendant's clutches as not a substantial factor in causing Mr. Stinson's asserted asbestos-related disease. The objections to Dr. Sawyer's declaration are sustained. The objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
